text.title: Ruby on Rails Framework Fundamentals Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure for hardening a Rails module to conventions.

step.name: audit-conventions
step.action: Run rails-convention-audit.sh. Count ad-hoc routes + callbacks-with-side-effects + missing strong params + secrets in ENV.

step.name: refactor-routes
step.action: Group endpoints by resource. Use resources blocks with member/collection. Rerun audit; ad-hoc count should drop.

step.name: fix-strong-params
step.action: Each controller defines a private `<resource>_params` method using `params.require(...).permit(...)`. Controller actions reference it.

step.name: move-side-effects-from-callbacks
step.action: For each `after_create :send_email` / `after_save :enqueue_job`, move the call to a Service that explicitly performs the action. Remove the callback.
step.decision-gate: If callback still triggers external side effect, STOP and move.

step.name: migrate-secrets
step.action: For each secret, add to `config/credentials.yml.enc` (via `EDITOR=... rails credentials:edit`); access via Rails.application.credentials. Remove from ENV-template / deploy script.
